results.title: Vida: Clone yourself. Let AI do the work before you ask - Product Hunt
results.content:
2.   Local-first memory. Your interaction history is stored on your own device whenever possible, so you stay in control of your data.

3.   Never used for model training. Your data is never used to train or improve our AI models.

When Vida connects to tools like Slack, Notion, or Figma, it only accesses the specific context required to complete the task you’ve asked it to perform. The goal is to make Vida genuinely useful while keeping your data private and fully under your control.

Maker [...] :

Reply Rescue · Prompt Rescue · Resume Rescue · Workspace Cleanup · Daily Wrap

Here’s what Vida looks like in real work:

Manager: “Why is this project delayed?”

Vida pulls context from Slack / Notion / Figma and drafts a ready-to-send reply.

Your prompt is unclear.

Vida turns it into a production-ready prompt instantly.

CV outdated.

Vida rebuilds it based on your latest experience and target role.

Desktop chaos.

Vida structures everything first, then cleans it in one click.

End of day.

Vida summarizes what actually moved forward — what’s done, what matters, what’s next.

We’re building Vida in public and will keep shipping every SOTA use case one by one.

Try it at vida.app

Help us shape what your AI clone should learn next.

Show more

results.title: Is it possible to get a copy of VIDA?
results.content:
> Chinese clone <$200 VIDA/DiCE is fine. Its a duplicate of the OEM diag tool. Actually, this is a pretty big grey industry these days for DIYers. Nearly all cars have Chinese made copy of factory diag tools. I have this Volvo one. Toyota one (Techstream, $35) and just ordered a VW one (VCDS $10). Couldn't find one for my dad's Dodge Town and Country though   
   >    
   >  One thing to note is the VIDA software usually comes on a mini disc with the DiCE diag tool. If your laptop is slot instead of tray loaded, might want to transfer it onto a full size disc first.  
   > [...] 1. 08-03-2013, 04:00 AM   #1

   Quartz
    View Profile
    View Forum Posts
    Private Message

   Quartz is offline  Member

   Join Date
   :   Aug 2013

   Posts
   :   60

   ## Unhappy Is it possible to get a copy of VIDA? (or change these settings some other way) [...] >    
   >  I have a cloned DICE from eBay. Running an HP 6220 (in desperate need of a battery), XP PRO, 2GB RAM, DVD optical drive. VIDA takes a long time to get running but I have used it to get into the cars systems to diagnose a parking sensor problem. It worked fine. I suspect that using it just that once paid for it. I have not tried to do any adjustments to the car like you want to do.

08-30-2019, 09:25 PM [...] alan jackson

02-01-2016, 09:03 AM

Not tried that torrent.  
   
 I am setting up a DICE/VIDA clone at the moment (trying to)  
   
 The file will be downloaded as an ISO file image which must be burnt tp DUAL LAYER DVD using something like Ashampoo Burner (free)  
   
 I have a working torrent for 2014D and also the patch file.  
   
 Now a silly question - Does the computer need to be on the internet when running diags on the car?  
   
 I am trying to run it for the first time and it is giving me a message "THE REQUESTED RESOURCE (VIDA) IS NOT AVAILABLE.   
   
   
   
   
 Has anyone had that torrent work? Must it be burned first to a DVD for it to be properly extracted? I cannot get it to extract.

02-01-2016, 02:45 PM [...] billr99

05-08-2014, 02:20 AM

This is the file: dvd\_root\Install\DVD\Vida\_dvdheader\Compinfo.ini  
 Find the RAM configuration section, this is what mine looks like after editing:  
   
 [RAM]  
 XPMinValue=500  
 VistaMinValue=2000  
 ErrorMess=Computer Memory (RAM) is below the required  
   
 In case it's not obvious, if you have it on a DVD you'll need to copy everything to the hard drive and edit there.  
   
 DYZ  
   
 Perfect! Thanks much!  
   
 Bill

results.title: Would You Clone Yourself?
results.content:
Sitemap

Open in app

Sign up

Sign in

 we are all up to date on the moral implications thanks to you reading this article. You’re welcome.

_Also, sincere thank you to all who responded! It certainly made the research and writing of this piece a lot more fun and insightful. Let me know if you’d like me to continue doing these in the future._

### Survey Results

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image 4

That’s a pretty resounding “no”!

This was expected, to be fair. There are a multitude of reasons why a clone of ourselves can make us uneasy. Let’s see what people had to say in support of their argument.

### Arguments against… [...] This is intriguing. A chance to see yourself from the side, not just in a mirror but in your interactions, mannerisms and so on. Would this be helpful to you if you found something that you think is an annoying habit to change? Or on the contrary, you realise that mannerism or part of your appearance you considered negative is actually not negative at all? Or, you become so caught up with over-analysing everything the clone does and become obsessed? Some of us are good enough at obsessing over ourselves when there’s just one version of us after all. There’s all sorts of psychological ramifications (I imagine) that we can’t predict or expect from seeing a version of ourselves living alongside us. But wouldn’t it be an interesting experiment to see anyway? I’m sure there’s a lot we can [...] > “Ultimately the clone would have nothing common with me apart from being a snapshot of me at one given moment. All commonalties I’d have with it would cease at the exact moment of cloning. As it would be another organism with its own consciousness and intentionality, its interpretation of the experiences and memories it inherited from me would surely be different from my own. So in a way, it wouldn’t be as much cloning as it would be simply creating a new person. Or would it?”
